Title: Caffeine-free hawk tea lowers cholesterol by reducing free cholesterol uptake and the production of very-low-density lipoprotein

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: HepG2; HL-7702; HeLa (reporter context); rat liver in vivo ortholog context
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: LDL clearance from circulation lowers cardiovascular risk
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dose- and time-dependently upregulated by HTE in HepG2 and HL-7702; increased in rat liver
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HTE induces LDLR transcription and protein abundance, promoting LDL clearance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; western blot; promoter luciferase assay; SRE motif mutation; 3'UTR reporter; 25-HC suppression experiments
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray, GEO: GSE117583; RNA-seq, GEO: GSE125084
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LDLR was one of the most strongly upregulated cholesterol-related genes after HTE treatment in vitro and in vivo. 2. HTE increased LDLR promoter activity through the SRE motif but not the 3'UTR. 3. EGCG reproduced LDLR induction, linking the effect to the NPC1L1-SREBP2 axis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to phenotype, LDLR is the downstream effector rather than the initiating target of HTE.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Human LDLR promoter in pGL3; constitutive reporter system

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: HepG2; Caco2; HeLa (reporter context); rat liver ortholog examined in vivo
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential structural component of VLDL/LDL affecting circulating cholesterol burden
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by HTE in HepG2 and Caco2; not altered in rat liver
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HTE reduces APOB expression/promoter activity and suppresses APOB-containing particle secretion in cultured cells
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time/dose studies; ELISA for APOB secretion; promoter luciferase with DR2 mutation/deletion; HNF4α overexpression rescue
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HTE reduced APOB mRNA and APOB-containing particle secretion in HepG2 and Caco2 cells. 2. HTE repression required the HNF4α-responsive DR2 element in the APOB promoter. 3. Regulation differed in vivo, as rat liver Apob was not significantly changed by HTE.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes species/model inconsistency between cultured human cells and rat liver for APOB regulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Therapeutic/nutraceutical target for lowering VLDL/LDL production
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Human APOB promoter in pGL3; constitutive reporter system

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with target-product reduction by supplying intracellular cholesterol pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major circulating lipid linked to cardiovascular disease risk
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Brush border membrane of intestinal enterocytes and canalicular membrane of hepatocytes in humans
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in HepG2/Caco2 context; not reduced by HTE
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates cellular uptake/internalization of free cholesterol rather than a catalytic chemical conversion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HTE and EGCG inhibit NPC1L1-mediated free cholesterol uptake, lowering intracellular cholesterol and contributing to serum cholesterol reduction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: NBD-cholesterol uptake assay by flow cytometry; NPC1L1-EGFP trafficking/confocal imaging; comparison with Ezetimibe; qRT-PCR showing expression unchanged
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: HTE treatment; EGCG treatment; cholesterol depletion/replenishment; Ezetimibe positive control
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HTE rapidly inhibited free cholesterol uptake in HepG2 and Caco2 cells. 2. HTE blocked cholesterol-replenishment-induced endocytosis of NPC1L1 from plasma membrane to endocytic recycling compartment, resembling Ezetimibe. 3. EGCG reproduced the NPC1L1 trafficking and cholesterol uptake phenotype, identifying it as a bioactive component for this axis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although NPC1L1 is a transporter rather than an enzyme, it is a primary mechanistic target in the Results. Expression was not reduced by HTE; the effect is via trafficking/endocytosis inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily pharmacological/nutraceutical target rather than plant breeding target; useful biomarker for screening cholesterol-lowering compounds

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hepatic and intestinal lipoprotein assembly module
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for assembly/storage-export of target lipoprotein product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Circulating lipoprotein precursor of LDL contributing to hypercholesterolemia
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hepatic and intestinal expression context inferred from HepG2 and Caco2 experiments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dose- and time-dependently downregulated by HTE in HepG2 and Caco2; reduced in rat liver
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Neutral lipids and newly synthesized APOB-containing particles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers lipids during assembly of APOB-containing lipoproteins in the ER
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HTE suppresses MTP expression, reducing VLDL production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; western blot; promoter luciferase assays with DR1/SRE mutation/deletion; HNF4α overexpression rescue; ELISA for APOB secretion
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray, GEO: GSE117583; RNA-seq, GEO: GSE125084
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HTE dose- and time-dependently reduced MTP mRNA and protein in HepG2 and Caco2 cells. 2. HTE reduced rat liver Mtp expression. 3. HTE repressed MTP promoter activity through the HNF4α-binding DR1 element, not through the SRE, and HNF4α overexpression rescued repression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MTP repression by HTE was sterol-independent because 25-HC did not alter the effect.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Direct MTP inhibitors can cause steatosis, but HTE did not show this phenotype in rats
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful therapeutic/nutraceutical target for lowering VLDL/LDL production

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH-leucine zipper sterol regulatory element-binding protein
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major circulating lipid linked to cardiovascular risk
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER membrane precursor; mature nuclear form
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated by HTE in HepG2 cells, with increased mature protein form
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation after proteolytic maturation under sterol depletion
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates LDLR and other sterol-regulated genes including HMGCR, HMGCS1, and FPP
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Intracellular sterol depletion caused by HTE-mediated inhibition of cholesterol uptake; suppressed by 25-HC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HTE activates SREBP2, which induces LDLR transcription and promotes LDL clearance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential mediator linking reduced cholesterol uptake to increased LDLR expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Western blot of precursor and mature SREBP2; LDLR promoter mutation of SRE motif; 25-HC suppression experiments; qRT-PCR of downstream genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray, GEO: GSE117583; RNA-seq, GEO: GSE125084
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HTE increased the mature form of SREBP2 while decreasing the precursor form. 2. Mutation of the SRE motif in the LDLR promoter abolished HTE responsiveness. 3. 25-HC blocked HTE-induced upregulation of LDLR and other SREBP2 target genes, showing sterol-dependent feedback activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study positions SREBP2 as a mediator rather than a direct target of tea constituents.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory node for cholesterol-lowering intervention
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: LDLR promoter reporter; constitutive transfection context

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: VLDL is precursor of circulating LDL and contributes to hypercholesterolemia
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally active in HepG2 and HeLa assays; HTE suppresses HNF4α-mediated transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of target promoters; activity suppressed by HTE and by kaempferol/quercetin
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MTP and APOB promoters via DR1 and DR2 elements; also regulates G6Pase and PEPCK
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: HTE treatment; kaempferol; quercetin; possible ligand binding to HNF4α ligand-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HTE suppresses HNF4α activity, reducing MTP/APOB expression and VLDL production
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream coordinator of the MTP/APOB axis controlling VLDL output
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter luciferase assays with DR1/DR2 deletion or mutation; HNF4α overexpression rescue; qRT-PCR of target genes; docking-supported ligand prediction; MTP protein and APOB secretion assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: HTE treatment; 25-HC cotreatment showed sterol independence; kaempferol and quercetin treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HTE inhibited MTP and APOB promoter activities through HNF4α-binding DR elements. 2. HNF4α overexpression rescued HTE-induced repression of MTP and APOB. 3. Kaempferol and quercetin reproduced HTE effects and were predicted as HNF4α ligands, supporting HNF4α as the regulatory node for reduced VLDL production.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers suppression of HNF4α activity rather than explicitly showing decreased HNF4A transcript/protein abundance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential therapeutic/nutraceutical target for lowering VLDL production
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: MTP and APOB promoter reporters; constitutive transfection context
